title: Lycianthes ledermannii
intro: Lycianthes ledermannii, also known as the purple passionflower or purple passionplant, is a flowering plant species native to New Guinea. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and pain. Bioactive constituents of Lycianthes ledermannii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Lycianthes ledermannii is a species of flowering plant in the nightshade family (Solanaceae). It belongs to the genus Lycianthes, which is a part of the larger family Solanaceae.
